nodes.label: The Transport Museum
nodes.description: Using maths for distance, time, and money in everyday travel and museum visits

nodes.label: Distance
nodes.description: How far one place is from another, measured in metres or kilometres

nodes.label: Units of Distance
nodes.description: Metres (m) for short distances, kilometres (km) for long ones; 1 km = 1000 m

nodes.label: Round Trip
nodes.description: Going and coming back; total distance = 2 x one-way distance

nodes.label: Time Calculations
nodes.description: Finding travel time, arrival time, and duration of events

nodes.label: Reading Time
nodes.description: Using hours and minutes; AM for morning, PM for afternoon and evening

nodes.label: Finding Duration
nodes.description: Subtract start time from end time; count hours first, then minutes

nodes.label: Schedules
nodes.description: Reading bus and train timetables to plan travel

nodes.label: Money Calculations
nodes.description: Working with rupees for tickets, fares, and shopping

nodes.label: Ticket Prices
nodes.description: Finding total cost: multiply price by number of tickets

nodes.label: Giving Change
nodes.description: Change = money paid - cost; always subtract to find change

nodes.label: Comparing Costs
nodes.description: Find how much more or less by subtracting the two prices

nodes.label: Types of Transport
nodes.description: Buses, trains, autos, cars, cycles - each with different fares and speeds

nodes.label: Fare Calculation
nodes.description: Some fares are fixed, some depend on distance; read the fare chart carefully

nodes.label: Word Problems
nodes.description: Using addition, subtraction, and multiplication to solve real-life museum problems

nodes.label: Problem-Solving Tip
nodes.description: Read carefully, find what is asked, pick the right operation, and check your answer
